controls.title: Policies and governance
controls.description: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

controls.title: Legal and regulatory requirements
controls.description: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.

controls.title: Trustworthy AI characteristics
controls.description: Trustworthy AI characteristics are integrated into organizational policies, procedures, and processes.

controls.title: Processes and procedures alignment
controls.description: Processes, procedures, and practices are in place to determine the needed level of risk management activities based on the organization's risk tolerance.

controls.title: Risk management process integration
controls.description: The risk management process and its outcomes are established through transparent policies, procedures, and other controls based on organizational risk priorities.

controls.title: Ongoing monitoring
controls.description: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk management process and its outcomes are planned, organizationally aligned, and documented.

controls.title: Mechanisms for inventory
controls.description: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and are resourced per organizational risk priorities.

controls.title: Processes for termination
controls.description: Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely and in a manner that does not increase risks or harms.

controls.title: Accountability structures
controls.description: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.

controls.title: Roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Roles and responsibilities and lines of communication related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks are documented and are clear to individuals and teams throughout the organization.

controls.title: Organizational commitment
controls.description: The organization's personnel and partners receive AI risk management training to enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies, procedures, and agreements.

controls.title: Executive responsibility
controls.description: Executive leadership of the organization takes responsibility for decisions about risks associated with AI system development and deployment.

controls.title: Workforce diversity
controls.description: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility processes are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks throughout the lifecycle.

controls.title: Decision-making diversity
controls.description: Decision-making related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks throughout the lifecycle is informed by a diverse team.

controls.title: Demographic diversity and domain expertise
controls.description: Policies and procedures are in place to define and differentiate roles and responsibilities for human-AI configurations and oversight of AI systems.

controls.title: Organizational commitments
controls.description: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk.

controls.title: Organizational practices and norms
controls.description: Organizational practices and norms that enable managing AI risk are integrated into risk management and align with established enterprise governance.

controls.title: Organizational teams commitment
controls.description: Organizational teams document the risks and potential impacts of the AI technology they design, develop, deploy, evaluate, and use, and they communicate about the impacts more broadly.

controls.title: Risk treatment approaches
controls.description: Organizational practices are in place to enable AI testing, identification of incidents, and information sharing.

controls.title: Stakeholder engagement
controls.description: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.

controls.title: Engagement with external stakeholders
controls.description: Organizational policies and practices are in place to engage with and foster collaboration between relevant AI actors.

controls.title: Mechanisms for feedback
controls.description: Mechanisms are established to enable AI actors to regularly incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into the design and implementation of AI systems.

controls.title: Policies for third-party entities
controls.description: Policies and procedures are in place that address AI risks associated with third-party entities, including risks of infringement of a third party's intellectual property or other rights.

controls.title: Third-party risk management
controls.description: Policies and procedures are in place that address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

controls.title: Contingency processes
controls.description: Contingency processes are in place for third-party AI systems or AI-enabled services.

controls.title: Context and intended purpose
controls.description: Context is established and understood.

controls.title: Intended purpose and setting
controls.description: Intended purpose, potentially beneficial uses, context of use, and the design, development, and deployment setting are understood.

controls.title: Interdisciplinary expertise
controls.description: Interdisciplinary AI actors, competencies, skills, and capacities for establishing context reflect the state of AI risks and are periodically reviewed.

controls.title: Broad set of perspectives
controls.description: The business value or context of business use has been clearly defined or—in the case of assessing existing AI systems—re-evaluated.

controls.title: Assessment of positive and negative impacts
controls.description: The organization's or system's mission and relevant goals for AI technology are documented.

controls.title: Use case risk assessment
controls.description: Organizational risk tolerances are determined and documented.

controls.title: System requirements
controls.description: System requirements (e.g., human-AI configuration, governance, legal compliance, privacy, safety, security, sustainability) are elicited from and understood by relevant AI actors.

controls.title: Categorize AI system
controls.description: Categorization of the AI system is performed.

controls.title: Intended use and known misuse
controls.description: The specific task, use case, or decision the AI system will be used for, and the expected benefit of the AI system is documented.

controls.title: Technology-specific risk
controls.description: Information about the AI system's knowledge limits and how system output may be utilized and overseen by humans is documented; this includes considerations of how output could be misused.

controls.title: Scientific integrity
controls.description: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented, including those related to experimental design, data collection and selection, and construct validity.

controls.title: Identify benefits and costs
controls.description: AI system benefits and costs are identified and managed to achieve trustworthy AI.

controls.title: Benefits and costs of AI
controls.description: Benefits of intended functionality are examined against the risks, and consideration of deployment is given against its benefits and compared to deployment of AI systems of lower risk.

controls.title: Potential costs
controls.description: Potential costs, including non-monetary costs, are examined and documented.

controls.title: Risk indicators
controls.description: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system, including third-party software and data.

controls.title: Risk mapping
controls.description: Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of its components — including the use of third-party data or software — are in place, followed, and documented, as are risks of infringement of a third party's intellectual property or other rights.

controls.title: Internal risk controls
controls.description: Internal risk controls for components of the AI system, including third-party AI technologies, are identified and documented.

controls.title: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities
controls.description: Likelihood and severity of each identified impact based on expected use, past uses, and system behavior is assessed and documented.

controls.title: Likelihood and severity
controls.description: Likelihood and severity of each identified impact based on expected use, past uses of AI systems in similar contexts, and AI system trustworthiness characteristics are assessed and documented.

controls.title: Impact assessment and documentation
controls.description: Practices and personnel for supporting regular engagement with relevant AI actors and integrating feedback about positive, negative, and unanticipated impacts are in place and documented.

controls.title: Appropriate methods and metrics
controls.description: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied.

controls.title: Measurement approaches
controls.description: Approaches and metrics for measurement of AI risks enumerated during the MAP function are selected for implementation starting with the most significant AI risks.

controls.title: Appropriateness of metrics
controls.description: Appropriateness of AI metrics and effectiveness of existing measures are regularly assessed and updated, including reports of errors and potential impacts on affected communities.

controls.title: Internal and external expert feedback
controls.description: Internal experts who did not serve as combating designers, developers, or deployers of the AI system and/or independent assessors are involved in regular assessments and updates.

controls.title: AI systems evaluated
controls.description: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.

controls.title: Test sets and metrics
controls.description: Test sets, metrics, and details about the tools used during test, evaluation, validation, and verification (TEVV) are documented.

controls.title: Evaluations for bias, security, privacy
controls.description: Evaluations involving AI actors and affected communities for validity, trustworthiness, and fitness for purpose of the AI system, model, or data are documented.

controls.title: AI system performance
controls.description: AI system performance or assurance criteria are measured qualitatively or quantitatively and demonstrated for conditions similar to deployment setting(s).

controls.title: Values and comparisons
controls.description: The functionality and behavior of the AI system and its components — as identified in the MAP function — are monitored when in production.

controls.title: Adversarial testing
controls.description: AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks — as identified in the MAP function. The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable. Limitations of the generalizability beyond the conditions under which the technology was developed are documented.

controls.title: AI system evaluation frequency
controls.description: The AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks — as identified in the MAP function. The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable.

controls.title: AI system security and resilience
controls.description: AI system security and resilience — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and documented.

controls.title: Bias and fairness
controls.description: Risks associated with transparency and accountability — as identified in the MAP function — are examined and documented.

controls.title: Environmental impact
controls.description: The AI model is explained, validated, and documented, and AI system output is interpreted within its context — as identified in the MAP function.

controls.title: Privacy risk
controls.description: Privacy risk of the AI system — as identified in the MAP function — is examined and documented.

controls.title: Fairness assessment
controls.description: Fairness and bias — Loss, validity, reliability, and robustness — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated, and results are documented.

controls.title: Mechanisms for tracking risks
controls.description: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place.

controls.title: Risk tracking and response
controls.description: Approaches, personnel, and documentation are in place to regularly identify and track existing, unanticipated, and emergent AI risks based on factors such as intended and actual performance in deployed contexts.

controls.title: Tracking risk over time
controls.description: Risk tracking approaches are considered for settings where AI risks are difficult to assess using currently available measurement techniques or metrics.

controls.title: Feedback mechanisms
controls.description: Feedback processes for end users and impacted communities to report problems and appeal system outcomes are established and integrated into AI system evaluation metrics.

controls.title: Measurement results
controls.description: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are informed by input from domain experts and relevant AI actors to validate whether the system is performing consistently as intended.

controls.title: Measurement results communication
controls.description: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are informed by input from domain experts and relevant AI actors to validate whether the system is performing consistently as intended.

controls.title: Measurement results documentation
controls.description: Measurement results are documented and used to make decisions and inform actions related to AI system development and deployment.

controls.title: Risk treatment plan
controls.description: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed.

controls.title: Risk treatment prioritization
controls.description: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purpose and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed.

controls.title: Ongoing risk treatment
controls.description: Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritized based on impact, likelihood, and available resources or methods.

controls.title: Responses proportional to risk
controls.description: Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority, as identified by the MAP function, are developed, planned, and documented. Risk response options can include mitigating, transferring, avoiding, or accepting.

controls.title: Risk treatment resources
controls.description: Negative residual risks (defined as the sum of all unmitigated risks) to both downstream acquirers of AI systems and end users are documented.

controls.title: Risk response and recovery
controls.description: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors.

controls.title: Risk response — planned
controls.description: Resources required to manage AI risks are taken into account — along with viable non-AI alternative systems, approaches, or methods — to reduce the magnitude or likelihood of potential impacts.

controls.title: Risk response — incident
controls.description: Mechanisms are in place and applied to sustain the value of deployed AI systems.

controls.title: Risk response — recovery
controls.description: Procedures are followed to respond to and recover from a previously unknown risk when it is identified.

controls.title: Risk response — change management
controls.description: Mechanisms are in place and applied, and responsibilities are assigned and understood, to supersede, disengage, or deactivate AI systems that demonstrate performance or outcomes inconsistent with intended use.

controls.title: Communication and documentation
controls.description: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are managed.

controls.title: Pre-deployment assessment
controls.description: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed, and processes and procedures for third-party risk management are in place.

controls.title: Communication of incidents
controls.description: Pre-trained models which are used for development are monitored as part of AI system regular monitoring and maintenance.

controls.title: Post-deployment monitoring
controls.description: Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans for the identified and measured AI risks are documented and monitored regularly.

controls.title: Post-deployment risk treatment
controls.description: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are implemented, including mechanisms for capturing and evaluating input from users and other relevant AI actors, appeal and override, decommissioning, incident response, recovery, and change management.

controls.title: Post-deployment monitoring results
controls.description: Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties, including relevant AI actors.

controls.title: AI system impact
controls.description: Impact assessment of shadow AI on organisational data security posture � ungoverned data flows quantified; Impact assessment of endpoint AI agents � data access scope, exfiltration paths, user risk

changelog.change: Initial seed — all GOVERN, MAP, MEASURE, MANAGE subcategories
